desires: Complete the term project before Thursday so I don't have to look incompetent in front of the professor.
Finish this tiny chore immediately and get it off my chest so I can stop thinking about it.

goals.description: Finish the term project draft tonight before midnight to secure an immediate productivity win and avoid facing the professor tomorrow.
goals.rationale: The deadline is still 3 days away, which feels safe enough that I don't need to panic and start working *right now*. But finishing it tonight gives me an immediate dopamine hit of 'productivity' without having to face the social friction of a morning meeting or explaining my lack of progress. It satisfies my academic value without demanding long-term planning.

goals.description: Do the trivial chore instantly right after the project is done, then immediately collapse on the couch to sleep.
goals.rationale: I only care about this if it provides an instant win before I give up completely. Since I'm currently avoiding the mental load of thinking about anything else, I'll wait until after I've already decided to quit trying for the day. If the chore takes longer than expected, I'll just drop it entirely rather than risk burning out before the project is even finished.
